Title: Django project scaffold
Description: Create the Django 5.x project with the four apps (games, puzzles, training, dashboard), env-driven settings (SQLite dev / Postgres prod, STOCKFISH_PATH), .env.example, ruff config, pytest-django wiring. No models yet.
Acceptance Criteria: runserver boots; pytest runs (zero tests) green; ruff check and format clean; .env is gitignored and .env.example documents every variable.

Title: Constants module + eval conversion
Description: The single constants module holding every §10 tunable, plus pure functions for the engine boundary: cp→win% sigmoid, side-to-move→user-perspective flip, mate-score clamp to 100/0. This is the H4 invariant made executable.
Acceptance Criteria: All 18 constants present, imported from exactly one module; table-driven tests cover cp→wp at extremes, perspective flips for both colours, and mate-in-N both directions.

Title: position_key normalization utility
Description: Pure function: FEN → normalized key. Strip halfmove/fullmove counters, keep ep square only when a legal ep capture exists (board.epd en_passant='legal' style), hash for the compact unique index.
Acceptance Criteria: Tests prove: same position with differing counters → same key; vestigial ep square after a double push with no legal ep capture → same key as without; genuinely different positions → different keys.

Title: games app models: Game + PipelineRun
Description: Game (with opponent_username, analysis bookkeeping, engine provenance fields) and PipelineRun per models.py. Migrations + admin registration with sensible list displays.
Acceptance Criteria: Migrations apply; admin can create/edit both models; Game unique on chesscom_uuid enforced.

Title: puzzles app models: MotifTag / Candidate / Puzzle / Occurrence / PuzzleMotif
Description: The core schema per models.py, including the Candidate gate ledger and per-solution-PV solutions JSON shape. Data migration seeds the 13 mechanism tags + OTHER with tiers.
Acceptance Criteria: Migrations apply; MotifTag seeded (14 rows); position_key unique constraint and (game, ply) uniqueness on Candidate and Occurrence enforced; admin registered for all five.

Title: training + dashboard models: Attempt / Report / WeaknessSnapshot
Description: Attempt (whole-line moves JSON with per-move verdicts, grade, hints_used, failed_at_ply), Report, WeaknessSnapshot per models.py. Migrations + admin.
Acceptance Criteria: Migrations apply; admin registered; a sample Attempt with a 3-move line round-trips through the JSONField.

Title: chess.com archives client
Description: Wrapper over the public archives API: list months, fetch a month, honour the immutability rule (cache earlier months, always re-fetch the current month). No DB writes here — returns parsed game dicts.
Acceptance Criteria: Fixture-based tests (recorded JSON, no network) for month listing and fetch; caching rule unit-tested; polite User-Agent set.

Title: PGN → Game field parser
Description: Parse chess.com PGN + game JSON into Game fields: user colour/ratings, opponent_username/rating, result, ECO/opening, time control/class, and %clk extraction from comments. clock_seconds is nullable — absence handled, never fabricated.
Acceptance Criteria: Fixture PGNs cover: white and black perspectives, a game with no %clk (daily/old), an unrated game; parser returns clean field dicts for all.

Title: ingest command
Description: Management command: update_or_create by chesscom_uuid, new games PENDING, --since=YYYY-MM flag, writes a PipelineRun row with counts and error text. Thin wrapper over a testable pipeline module.
Acceptance Criteria: Running twice over the same months is a no-op (idempotency test asserts identical counts); --since limits the fetch window; PipelineRun row records games fetched/created/updated.

Title: Engine wrapper
Description: python-chess engine session manager: movetime-driven single-PV evaluation, MultiPV=3 probe, depth-SHALLOW_DEPTH probe. Converts every score to user-perspective win% at this boundary using M1-02 — raw engine scores never escape. Includes a scripted fake-engine test double for unit tests downstream.
Acceptance Criteria: @pytest.mark.engine integration test runs against real Stockfish; unit tests via the double verify perspective flips on both colours and mate clamping; engine process reused across a run, cleanly closed.

Title: Candidate detection sweep
Description: Phase-1 pass: wp curve over every ply, flag AVOID candidates (user wp drop ≥ MISTAKE_WP_DROP) and PUNISH candidates per the §4 spec (opponent handed ≥ BLUNDER_WP_DROP; user's reply realized < PUNISH_CAPTURE_FRACTION of it). Phase and clock-bucket classification for each candidate.
Acceptance Criteria: Table-driven tests with the fake engine: AVOID and PUNISH detection, the punished-adequately non-candidate case, and the perspective arithmetic for both colours.

Title: Gates 1, 2, 4 + Candidate persistence
Description: Gate 1 (salvageable), gate 2 (shallow-depth findability + cashout, needs the shallow probe), gate 4 (trivial/forced/book). Every evaluated candidate persists as a Candidate row with gate evidence, verdict, and rejection_gate — pass or fail.
Acceptance Criteria: Table-driven (position facts → verdict) tests per gate; a rejected candidate leaves a full-evidence row; opening-leak promotion query over Candidates fires on the 3rd distinct game.

Title: Gate 3: solution set + per-solution PVs
Description: MultiPV probe → uniqueness gap, SOLUTION_BAND_WP band, MAX_SOLUTIONS cap, and a principal variation captured per accepted solution (the H3 fix). Solutions JSON matches the models.py shape exactly.
Acceptance Criteria: Tests: single clear solution, two band-mates each with their own PV, >2 acceptable moves → discard; uniqueness_gap_wp stored on the candidate.

Title: Puzzle/Occurrence writer + collision policy
Description: get_or_create Puzzle by position_key; solutions freeze at first analysis, deeper movetime may overwrite engine facts, PUNISH wins puzzle_type ties (logged); update_or_create Occurrence on (game, ply); quality score computed and recomputed on new occurrences; opening-leak flag set via the promotion query.
Acceptance Criteria: Tests: same position from two games → one Puzzle, two Occurrences; deeper re-analysis overwrites, shallower doesn't; AVOID/PUNISH collision keeps PUNISH; quality score changes when an occurrence is added.

Title: Static exchange evaluation (SEE)
Description: Careful SEE implementation (python-chess has no one-liner): iterative capture resolution on a square with least-valuable-attacker ordering, x-rays, and promotion awareness. Pure function, no DB.
Acceptance Criteria: Tested against a committed table of known exchange sequences (from published SEE test suites) including x-ray and pinned-attacker cases; all programmatically verified, no invented positions.

Title: Tier-1 detectors A: hanging piece + counting error
Description: SEE-based detectors: a piece en prise with negative exchange (hanging), and a capture sequence the user entered that comes out negative (counting error → direction MISCOUNTED).
Acceptance Criteria: Fixture FENs with engine-verified answers, committed; both detectors return tag + direction; no Stockfish calls in unit tests.

Title: Tier-1 detectors B: fork, pin, skewer, discovered attack
Description: Ray-logic detectors over the position + solution move. Pure functions taking (board, solution) → set of mechanism tags.
Acceptance Criteria: Fixture FENs per motif, both as the tactic-exists and tactic-absent case; detectors are pure and independently testable.

Title: Tier-1 detectors C: back-rank, trapped piece, mate threat, promotion/passed pawn + direction classifier
Description: Remaining rule detectors plus the MISSED/ALLOWED/MISCOUNTED direction classification (exactly one per puzzle, derived from puzzle type and detector output).
Acceptance Criteria: Fixture FENs per motif; direction classifier table-driven tests cover all three outcomes.

Title: analyze command
Description: The DB-as-queue command: query PENDING games, one transaction per game (engine pass → candidates → gates → puzzles/occurrences → detectors → ANALYZED with version fields). FAILED + three-strikes, --retry-failed, --reanalyze [--before-pipeline-version], --movetime. PipelineRun row with counts.
Acceptance Criteria: Crash mid-run (simulated) leaves N fully-analyzed games and the rest PENDING; re-run resumes and is idempotent; --reanalyze resets and reprocesses under the collision policy; yield on a real sample is order-of-magnitude ~1–3 puzzles/game.

Title: calibrate command
Description: --sample=50: dump random surviving puzzles to a static HTML page — board diagram (python-chess SVG), prompt, solution + PV, gate evidence, quality score, game context — for eyeballing.
Acceptance Criteria: Page renders 50 puzzles standalone (no server needed); each shows enough evidence to judge fair/unfair at a glance.

Title: Backfill + calibration session
Description: Run ingest + analyze over full history (niced, overnight, --movetime recorded). Review the calibration page: unfair → raise findability bar; pointless → raise gate 1. Re-filter via Candidates, iterate. Record every constant change + observation in Design.md §10. This is the checkpoint — M5/M6 do not start until puzzles feel fair.
Acceptance Criteria: Full history ANALYZED; at least one review iteration documented in §10; explicit go/no-go note recorded; CLAUDE.md checkboxes 1–4 ticked.

Title: sm2_update pure function
Description: SM-2 in its own module: (state, correct, latency_ms, hints_used) → new state. Fail → lapse (repetitions reset, interval ≈ 1 day, ease −0.2, floor 1.3); success grades by latency/hints (clean <30s → 5, slow/hinted → 3–4). Touches nothing.
Acceptance Criteria: Exhaustive unit tests: every grade path, lapse behaviour, ease floor, interval growth over simulated multi-review sequences; zero imports from Django.

Title: Static assets + base templates + auth
Description: Vendor chessground + chess.js (no npm build), base layout template, login middleware with single superuser — the whole app behind a lock.
Acceptance Criteria: Assets served by Django staticfiles; an unauthenticated request to any page redirects to login; board renders in a scratch template.

Title: GET /train/next
Description: Serving query: overdue by (due_at, -quality_score); buried excluded; when clear, introduce new puzzles by quality capped at NEW_PUZZLES_PER_DAY; soft-mix PUNISH/AVOID. Response includes orientation and the context block (date, opponent, clock, occurrence count).
Acceptance Criteria: Unit tests on the queryset: ordering, bury exclusion, daily-cap counting via today's first-attempts, empty-queue response; context block fields all present.

Title: POST /train/attempt
Description: Stateless validation: replay the submitted full move list from the puzzle FEN via python-chess; first move against the solution set, later moves against that solution's PV; near-misses logged per-move; opponent replies returned from the PV; grade derived (latency + hints) and sm2_update applied; Attempt row written with the whole line.
Acceptance Criteria: Tests: correct line (both solutions if two), wrong first move, PV deviation mid-line logged as near-miss, cashout cap respected, illegal move list rejected; SM-2 state visibly updates; client-side legality remains UX-only.

Title: Train widget UI
Description: ~150 lines of vanilla JS: board wiring, move submission, server-driven opponent replies, two-stage hints (piece then motif), completion flow, and the fail screen — full line replay, explanation, motif tags, link to the real game. The fail screen is the product.
Acceptance Criteria: Full solve and full fail both work end-to-end in a browser; hints visibly price into the grade; fail screen shows all four elements; UK English copy.

Title: bury + report endpoints
Description: POST bury (30 days, touches buried_until only — never SM-2 state or stats) and POST report (Report row, the ongoing calibration signal), with minimal UI hooks on the train page.
Acceptance Criteria: Buried puzzle vanishes from /train/next until expiry with SM-2 fields unchanged; report creates a row visible in admin.

Title: snapshot command
Description: Nightly delete-and-recompute of WeaknessSnapshot per motif: trailing-window occurrence counts from games (via Candidates/Occurrences) and attempt/correct counts from training. PipelineRun row.
Acceptance Criteria: Running twice for the same date yields identical rows (idempotent); rejected-candidate mistakes count on the games side; window boundaries tested.

Title: /dashboard page
Description: Due count, streak, trend charts (Chart.js over small JSON endpoints), and the motif table showing the games-vs-training gap — still blundering it in games vs solving it when drilled.
Acceptance Criteria: Page renders from real data; gap column shows both rates side by side; JSON endpoints return only aggregates (no request-path recomputation of history).

Title: /openings + /puzzles pages
Description: Opening-leak table (recurring misplayed positions with ECO/opening, recurrence count, wp cost) and the filterable puzzle archive (motif, phase, type, clock bucket).
Acceptance Criteria: Leak table ordered by recurrence × cost; archive filters combine; both server-rendered templates.

Title: /games health page
Description: Ingest/analysis status per game plus PipelineRun history — the pipeline-health page from §7.
Acceptance Criteria: Shows per-stage last run, status, counts, and error text; FAILED games listed with their errors.

Title: LLM client + batch prompt + schema validation
Description: Batched (10–15 puzzles/call) Tier-2 tagging via Claude headless (claude -p with OAuth token) or API: strict JSON schema for proposed tags + one-sentence explanation; invalid responses rejected whole. Never in the request path.
Acceptance Criteria: Schema validation rejects malformed output in tests (recorded fixtures, no live LLM in unit tests); batch assembly includes position, played move, solution, and game context.

Title: Tag verification layer
Description: Proposed tags with deterministic checkers verified against the board via the Tier-1 detectors; contradictions dropped, not stored; PuzzleMotif rows written with source=llm, confidence, rule_verified; tag_attempts three-strikes skip.
Acceptance Criteria: Tests: verifiable-and-correct tag stored, verifiable-and-wrong tag dropped, uncheckable tag stored with rule_verified NULL; a poison puzzle is skipped after TAG_MAX_ATTEMPTS and surfaced in counts.

Title: tag command + run_pipeline wrapper
Description: Management command over tagged_at IS NULL queue; tagged_at set only on joint success of tags + explanation; PipelineRun row. Plus the run_pipeline wrapper chaining ingest → analyze → tag.
Acceptance Criteria: Failed batch self-requeues (tagged_at stays NULL); success sets tagged_at once; disabling tag leaves the app fully functional; run_pipeline runs all three stages with three PipelineRun rows.

Title: Lightsail deployment
Description: Deploy alongside the existing RAG service: Postgres, app server, env/secrets (no secrets in repo), STOCKFISH_PATH, migrations, static files.
Acceptance Criteria: App reachable over HTTPS behind login; migrations applied against Postgres; deployment steps documented in the repo.

Title: cron + ops wiring
Description: The two crontab lines from §7: separate flock files, nice/ionice on the pipeline job, per-job healthchecks.io pings. Production backfill if not already run in M4.
Acceptance Criteria: Both jobs fire on schedule; overlapping-run test (long fake job) shows the lock skipping without starving the other job; healthchecks green; silence pages.
